critical.mandate: Product brief must exist before starting
Requirements must be discoverable, not guessed
Each requirement must have testable acceptance criteria

step.title: Load Product Brief
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/product-brief.md
Extract vision, target users, problem statement, proposed solution

step.title: User Interviews
step.ask: Who are the primary user types?
What frustrates them most about current solutions?
step.action: Structure responses into user need statements

step.title: Functional Requirements
step.action: List features organized by priority: Must-Have, Should-Have, Nice-to-Have
Each feature needs: description, user story format, acceptance criteria

step.title: Non-Functional Requirements
step.action: Define: performance targets, security requirements, accessibility standards
Define: scalability needs, reliability targets, compliance requirements

step.title: User Journeys
step.action: Map key user flows with happy path and error paths
Include: entry point, steps, decision points, exit conditions

step.title: Data Requirements
step.action: Identify data stored, processed, and exchanged
Define data retention, privacy, and security policies

step.title: Integration Requirements
step.action: List external systems, APIs, and third-party services
Define integration patterns and data exchange formats

step.title: Constraints and Assumptions
step.action: Document technical, budget, timeline, and team constraints
List all assumptions that requirements depend on

step.title: Success Criteria
step.action: Define measurable acceptance criteria for each feature
Define overall product success metrics

step.title: Generate Output
step.template-output: Generate PRD with all sections: overview, user personas, functional requirements (prioritized), non-functional requirements, user journeys, data requirements, integrations, constraints, assumptions, success criteria.

step.title: Adversarial Review
step.ask: Open a new Claude Code terminal and run /gaia-adversarial targeting {planning_artifacts}/prd.md. This is required — findings will be incorporated back into the PRD. Confirm here once it completes. (ok)
step.action: Wait for user to confirm adversarial-review-{date}.md exists in {planning_artifacts}/

step.title: Incorporate Adversarial Findings
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/adversarial-review-{date}.md — extract critical and high severity findings
For each critical/high finding: add as a new requirement or refine an existing requirement in the PRD
Add a "## Review Findings Incorporated" section to the PRD listing each finding, its severity, and how it was addressed (new requirement added / existing requirement refined / acknowledged as risk)
step.template-output: Update the PRD with adversarial review findings incorporated. Critical/high findings become new requirements or refine existing ones. Add "Review Findings Incorporated" section.
